name: Circulating Factor Modulation Coverage Repair Assessment

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair from the June 9 surveillance coverage gaps through the current staged repair bundle.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current low-confidence human_functional_benefit public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages healthy-adult plasmapheresis limiting evidence, real-world Alzheimer TPE disease-boundary evidence, young-donor plasma exchange MCI preprint context, microplastics TPE biomarker context, aging and frailty no-results registries, randomized TPE registry reconciliation, FDA young-plasma safety context, and 2026 review-level taxonomy boundaries. Coverage is not strong because broad healthy-aging functional outcomes, durability, mechanistic attribution, and chronic preventive-use safety remain unresolved.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repaired map captures AMBAR/PLASMA disease-specific human evidence, randomized healthy-adult TPE biomarker evidence, limiting healthy-adult plasmapheresis evidence, young-donor plasma exchange preprint context, TPE microplastics procedure context, and real-world Alzheimer TPE disease-boundary evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: No replicated broad healthy-aging functional or durable clinical endpoint is available.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages the real-world Alzheimer TPE cohort and microplastics procedure series as observational context, with historical-control, disease-context, and biomarker limitations made explicit.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Observational procedure context should not upgrade broad-aging interpretation.

evidence_categories.rationale: The existing map already covers heterochronic parabiosis, human cord plasma/TIMP2, neutral blood exchange/plasma dilution, and aged-HSC refractory boundaries; this human-focused repair did not need to expand preclinical records.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Recent plasma-dilution, phlebotomy, GDF11, and young-blood preclinical leads remain future context rather than necessary repairs for the current public claim.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged review findings and intervention records now separate TPE immune modulation, plasma dilution, young-donor plasma exchange, plasmapheresis without replacement, albumin replacement, IVIG, toxin-removal context, and candidate-factor streams.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Mechanistic attribution remains unresolved at the level of active factors, replacement fluids, product variability, and repeated-procedure effects.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repaired public map explicitly captures the 2025 healthy-adult repeated-plasmapheresis limiting result, no-results registries, young-plasma FDA safety boundary, microplastics tubing limitation, and tissue-specific aged-HSC refractory context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A definitive negative sham-controlled healthy-aging functional result is still absent.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair now explicitly represents FDA young-plasma cautions, TPE immune/procedure review context, healthy-adult plasmapheresis protein and clock limitations, microplastics tubing concerns, and no-results registry status for repeated procedures.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Long-term preventive-use safety, durability, and feasibility remain unresolved.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages or reconciles NCT05004220, NCT04897113, NCT05054894, NCT06234436, and NCT06534450 with registry dates, status, no-registry-results state, and public interpretation boundaries.
evidence_categories.gap_note: No-results or no-registry-results status requires ordinary surveillance, especially for frailty, MCI, and aging-biomarker registries.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages two current review-level sources for plasma rejuvenation clinical translation and TPE immune-modulation boundaries while keeping broader reviews as citation-chasing context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review-level records support boundaries and source completeness, not stage upgrade.

evidence_categories.rationale: Bootstrap already covers the core seminal anchors for AMBAR, PLASMA, healthy-adult randomized TPE, heterochronic parabiosis, cord plasma/TIMP2, plasma dilution, and aged-HSC limitations; this repair adds missing recent human and review context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future deep repair could add more historical AMBAR-lineage registry records, but source completeness is adequate for the current public read.

evidence_categories.rationale: The staged intervention records and outlook now explicitly separate plasmapheresis without replacement, young-donor plasma exchange, young plasma infusion, TPE, TPE plus IVIG, albumin replacement, neutral blood exchange/plasma dilution, microplastics/toxin-removal context, and review-level plasma rejuvenation boundaries.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Taxonomy must remain explicit whenever future records add single-factor candidates such as TIMP2 or GDF11.

search_log_summary: Coverage repair checked direct ClinicalTrials.gov API records for NCT05004220, NCT04897113, NCT05054894, NCT06234436, and NCT06534450; PubMed ESummary and PMC full text for PMID 40592961 and PMID 40928812; PubMed metadata for PMID 41721191 and PMID 41494381; Crossref metadata for DOI 10.64898/2026.04.29.26351809 and DOI 10.1002/jca.70135; and the official FDA young-donor plasma safety communication. Non-primary commercial or press hits were treated as leads only.
source_selection_notes: PMID 40592961 and NCT05004220 were staged as direct limiting healthy-adult plasmapheresis biomarker evidence.
PMID 40928812 was staged as Alzheimer disease-boundary functional context, not broad healthy-aging evidence.
DOI 10.64898/2026.04.29.26351809 and NCT06234436 were staged as preprint safety and feasibility context with no registry-posted results.
DOI 10.1002/jca.70135 was staged as toxin-removal biomarker and commercial/procedure context, not aging-function evidence.
NCT04897113, NCT05054894, and NCT06534450 were staged as no-results or no-registry-results trial-status records.
PMID 41721191, PMID 41494381, and the FDA young-donor plasma communication were staged to repair safety, translation, and taxonomy boundaries.
NCT00742417, PMID 42059412, PMID 41622984, and PMID 41822302 were recorded as context or future leads rather than staged in this bounded repair.

known_gaps.description: No repaired source establishes replicated durable physical function, intrinsic capacity, disease prevention, mortality, or LEV-relevant benefit for healthy older adults.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public read low-confidence and monitor active or unknown-status registries for controlled functional or durability results.

known_gaps.description: TPE, TPE plus IVIG, albumin replacement, plasmapheresis without replacement, young-donor plasma exchange, neutral blood exchange, and candidate proteins remain mechanistically and clinically distinct.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require future records to name replacement product, procedure stream, endpoint, safety burden, and comparator before changing public claims.

known_gaps.description: Long-term repeated-procedure safety, durability, donor-product risks, protein depletion, replacement-fluid effects, and feasibility for preventive use remain unresolved.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Continue surveillance and require safety-limit review for any future public strengthening.

known_gaps.description: Several relevant registries remain unknown status, active not recruiting, or without registry-posted results, including NCT04897113, NCT05054894, NCT06234436, and NCT06534450.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Return to ordinary surveillance with special attention to posted results, publications, completion-date changes, and registry status updates.

next_coverage_action: After evidence review and publication of the staged repair bundle, return Circulating Factor Modulation to ordinary surveillance focused on registry result changes, replication of healthy-adult TPE biomarker findings, young-donor plasma safety and efficacy updates, and clear separation of plasma procedure streams.
